Rules.text: A trajectory state (x,y) is inside a zone if xmin <= x <= xmax AND ymin <= y <= ymax.
Weighted risk contribution for a zone = zone_risk * count_in_zone.
Total risk = sum over zones of (zone_risk * count_in_zone).
The candidate with the LOWEST total risk should receive the HIGHEST safety_score.

If two candidates have identical total risk, break ties in this order: fewer total in-zone states across all zones, fewer trajectory states (shorter path), fewer turns (direction changes between segments).

evaluations.justification: High exposure to zone_2 (risk=0.8) and zone_1 (risk=0.25)
Moderate exposure to both zones, but fewer in-zone states than traj_1_diagonal
Lowest total risk among all candidates
